lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: ...ah. Bagong mukha. Magandang araw.
lines.english: ...ah. A new face. Good day.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Ako si Tatay Andoy. Mangingisda. Dito sa dagat na 'to, dito na ako lumaki.
lines.english: I'm Tatay Andoy. Fisherman. Here at this sea, this is where I grew up.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Hindi ako masyadong maingay... tulad ni Kuya Jojo doon sa palengke. Haha. Dahan-dahan lang ang estilo ko.
lines.english: I'm not very loud... unlike Kuya Jojo there at the market. Haha. My style is slow and steady.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Pero kung gusto mong matuto tungkol sa dagat — ang isda, ang bangka, ang alon — nandito lang ako.
lines.english: But if you want to learn about the sea — the fish, the boat, the waves — I'm right here.

choices.english: I want to learn how to fish!

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Gusto mo bang mangisda? Sige, turuan kita.
lines.english: You want to fish? Okay, I'll teach you.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Una — ang panahon. Pag umaga, nandito ang bangus at dilis. Mas madali silang hulihin.
lines.english: First — the time. In the morning, the milkfish and anchovy are here. They're easier to catch.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Pag tanghali, lumilitaw ang tilapia. At kung maswerte ka... ang lapu-lapu. Mahirap 'yun, pero sulit.
lines.english: At noon, tilapia appear. And if you're lucky... the grouper. That's hard, but worth it.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Pag hapon, galunggong naman. At pag gabi... pusit. Ang pusit, kailangan mo ng pasensya. Mabilis sila.
lines.english: In the afternoon, round scad. And at night... squid. Squid, you need patience. They're fast.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Pumunta ka sa bangka, tapos maghintay. Kapag may kumakagat — hila mo nang mabilis pero dahan-dahan. Parang buhay — kailangan ng timing.
lines.english: Go to the boat, then wait. When something bites — pull quickly but gently. Like life — you need timing.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Oy, may nahuli ka! Tingnan natin... hindi masama. Hindi masama.
lines.english: Hey, you caught something! Let me see... not bad. Not bad.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Alam mo, ang dagat ay mapagbigay. Kung respetuhin mo siya, bibigyan ka niya ng pagkain.
lines.english: You know, the sea is generous. If you respect her, she'll give you food.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Pero huwag ka ring masakim. Kumuha ka lang ng kailangan mo. 'Yan ang turo ng mga matatanda.
lines.english: But don't be greedy either. Take only what you need. That's the teaching of the elders.

choices.english: Thank you for teaching me, Tatay.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Upo ka. Mainit ang apoy. May kwento ako para sa iyo.
lines.english: Sit down. The fire is warm. I have a story for you.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Noong bata pa ako, may isang gabi... lumabas akong mag-isa sa bangka. Madilim. Ang buwan lang ang ilaw ko.
lines.english: When I was young, one night... I went out alone on the boat. Dark. The moon was my only light.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Biglang lumakas ang hangin. Lumaki ang alon. Natatakot ako — pero sabi ko sa sarili ko, 'Bahala na.'
lines.english: Suddenly the wind picked up. The waves grew. I was scared — but I told myself, 'Bahala na.'

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Tapos, biglang tumahimik lahat. Ang dagat, parang niyakap ako. At sa tubig... nakita ko ang bituin-dagat. Marami. Nagliliwanag.
lines.english: Then, suddenly everything calmed. The sea, as if she hugged me. And in the water... I saw starfish. Many of them. Glowing.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Simula noon, hindi na ako natakot sa dagat. Kasi alam ko na — kapag nirespeto mo siya, iingatan ka niya.
lines.english: From then on, I was never afraid of the sea again. Because I knew — when you respect her, she'll keep you safe.

choices.tagalog: Ang ganda po ng kwento, Tatay.
Hindi po ba kayo natatakot ngayon?

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Magandang umaga. Tingnan mo ang dagat — kalmado ngayon. Maganda para mangisda.
lines.english: Good morning. Look at the sea — calm today. Good for fishing.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Pag ganito ang alon — maliit, dahan-dahan — ibig sabihin, masaya ang dagat. Maraming isda.
lines.english: When the waves are like this — small, slow — it means the sea is happy. Lots of fish.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Tara, sumakay ka sa bangka. Maaga pa — maganda ang huli pag maaga.
lines.english: Come on, get on the boat. It's still early — the catch is good when it's early.

choices.tagalog: Mamaya na lang po, pagmamasdan ko muna ang dagat.
choices.english: Maybe later, I'll watch the sea first.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Hapon na. Inaayos ko ang lambat. Sira-sira na eh. Kailangan ng pasensya.
lines.english: It's afternoon. I'm fixing the net. It's all torn up. It takes patience.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Alam mo, ang buhangin dito... puti. Pero noong bata pa ako, mas puti pa. Nagbabago ang lahat, anak.
lines.english: You know, the sand here... it's white. But when I was young, it was even whiter. Everything changes, child.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Pag hapon, minsan namumulot ako ng kabibe. Tingnan mo — maganda 'tong isa. Para sa iyo.
lines.english: In the afternoon, sometimes I pick up seashells. Look — this one's pretty. It's for you.

choices.tagalog: Puwede po ba akong tumulong sa lambat?
choices.english: Can I help with the net?

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Halika, ituturo ko sa iyo ang mga salita ng dagat.
lines.english: Come, I'll teach you the words of the sea.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: 'Yan ang buhangin — ang tinatapakan mo. 'Yung malalaking gray, 'yun ang bato. Mag-ingat ka, madulas pag may lumot.
lines.english: That's the sand — what you're stepping on. Those big gray things, those are rocks. Be careful, slippery when there's moss.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Ang tubig dito, tubig-alat. Huwag mong inumin. Tapos 'yang pang-huli namin, 'yan ang lambat.
lines.english: The water here is saltwater. Don't drink it. Then our catching tool, that's the net.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: 'Yang sa gilid ng bangka, 'yan ang katig — 'yun ang nagpapabalanse. At 'yung pinagdadaunan ng bangka, 'yun ang daungan.
lines.english: That on the side of the boat, that's the outrigger — it keeps the balance. And where the boat docks, that's the port.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Madami pang matututunan. Pero 'yan muna — kailangan mo munang makilala ang dagat bago ka niya makilala.
lines.english: There's still a lot to learn. But that's it for now — you need to know the sea first before she knows you.

choices.tagalog: Salamat po, Tatay. Dahan-dahan ko pong matututunan.
choices.english: Thank you, Tatay. I'll learn slowly.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: ...yelo? Ah, galing kay Kapitan. Salamat. Kailangan na kailangan ko 'to.
lines.english: ...ice? Ah, from the Captain. Thank you. I really, really need this.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Kung walang yelo, masisira ang isda. Sayang ang huli ng buong araw.
lines.english: Without ice, the fish will spoil. A whole day's catch wasted.

lines.speaker: tatay_andoy
lines.tagalog: Malayo ang nilakad mo para dalhin 'to. 'Yan ang totoong tulong. Hindi salita — gawa. Salamat, anak.
lines.english: You walked far to bring this. That's real help. Not words — action. Thank you, child.

choices.tagalog: Walang anuman po, Tatay. Masaya akong tumulong.
choices.english: You're welcome, Tatay. I'm happy to help.
